actionUnits.condition: Trigger only after confirming that 'Teleport Area Setup' or its children expose a Collider or EventTrigger, or have a MonoBehaviour whose class exposes UnityEvents (m_Calls). The MonoBehaviour component settings present in the scene file are treated as the exact configuration of the attached script. Once the MonoBehaviour(s) and their m_Calls (with script fileIDs) are provided for the root and children (IDs observed: 1565887664197881479/1565887664197881481 and 756086973/1071784861), we will invoke the configured no-argument public methods to simulate the Teleport-area interaction event.

Manually simulate XR hover/exit and teleport to drive AnchorVisuals because no XR interactor is present in the harness. The MonoBehaviour component (fileID: 3953368309659336407) in the scene meta is the exact configuration of the provided AnchorVisuals script (guid: b2dd7fabdb14fca498bd9e0def11ab19) attached to child PrefabInstance 'Teleport Anchor' (m_PrefabInstance: 1071784861) under 'Teleport Area Setup' (Transform fileID: 1565887663878291441). Fields bound via scene meta: m_FadeAnimation=8970193181346821320, m_Arrow=8134302612814971439, m_TargetArrowHeight=1, m_ArrowAnimationDuration=0.375, m_AnimationCurve serialized. Trigger the public no-arg methods to ensure code coverage of OnAnchorEnter/OnAnchorExit/HideArrowOnTeleport.
